title: Ageratina tomentella
intro: Ageratina tomentella, also known as Mexican coffee plant, is a shrub-like plant native to the Americas, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its extracts have been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Ageratina tomentella include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ageratina tomentella is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to treat digestive issues, colds, and coughs, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
taxonomy: Ageratina tomentella is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is commonly known as a member of the genus Ageratina, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. The plant's classification includes being a part of the tribe Eupatorieae. This tribe is further classified under the subfamily Asteroideae. Ageratina tomentella's classification also includes being a member of the order Asterales.
uses: Ageratina tomentella, also known as the "Fern-leaf Mountain Silverbeard", has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of its native range. It is believed to possess antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating infections and wounds. The plant's extracts are also thought to have anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Additionally, Ageratina tomentella has been used to treat fever and digestive issues. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Ageratina tomentella, also known as Guzmania, has been traditionally used for medicinal purposes, but its use can lead to various side effects. Some reported side effects include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can cause stomach problems, nausea, and vomiting.
